title: prunus divaricata subsp. pontica
intro: Prunus divaricata subsp. pontica, also known as Pontic cherry or Caucasian cherry, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae. It is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Prunus divaricata subsp. pontica include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and boost the immune system, making it a popular herbal remedy in traditional medicine. Prunus divaricata subsp. pontica is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and can also be used in the form of dried flowers, leaves, and bark for various herbal preparations.
